pattern: A single post-order traversal that returns each subtree's sum and node count lets every node test its own value against its floored subtree average in one pass.
hints: Each node's check needs the sum and size of its own subtree, which its children can hand up.
Compute results bottom-up: a node's subtree sum and count are its value plus the totals from both children.
During the post-order return, compare the node's value to floor(subtreeSum / subtreeCount) and increment a shared counter on a match.

edge_inputs.description: flooring makes a parent match its rounded-down average
node value differs from non-integer average
all zeros so every node matches

stress.description: random near-complete tree from a dense level-order array
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1000)))
    vals = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [vals]
stress.note: a dense array is a valid level-order tree with no missing internal slots
